SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.util.scenario

Class AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.util.scenario.AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark
public class AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark extends Object
Reproducible dynamic benchmark for the AntiSurgeController recycle-valve loop.

HYSYS/UniSim Dynamics are the industrial reference for compressor trip and anti-surge tuning because they couple proven valve/actuator dynamics to the gas-path mass-storage. NeqSim's AntiSurgeController is a tested PI anti-surge law; what was missing was a documented, repeatable transient case in which the controller is exercised against a moving surge margin so its dynamic response (proportional kick, integral action, anti-windup, valve actuation) can be verified and tuned before field commissioning.

This benchmark provides exactly that. It drives a real AntiSurgeController and a real ThrottlingValve against a transparent first-order gas-path surrogate: a flow-reduction (trip-like) disturbance steadily erodes the compressor distance to surge, while opening the recycle valve restores it. The plant model is

$$ m_{k+1} = m_k - \dot d\,\Delta t + a\,\frac{u_k}{100}\,\Delta t $$

where \(m\) is the distance to surge, \(\dot d\) is the disturbance rate (per second), \(a\) is the recycle authority (per second at fully open valve) and \(u\) is the valve opening in percent. This is the same class of lumped surrogate used to tune anti-surge controllers ahead of a full dynamic study; it is not validated field data and is not a substitute for a vendor dynamic model, but it gives a deterministic, inspectable benchmark with an analytically known expected behaviour (the closed loop must hold the margin near the set point; the open loop must surge).

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    private static final class 
    AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark.BenchmarkCompressor
    Minimal Compressor subclass that exposes a directly settable distance to surge so the benchmark can impose a transparent gas-path surrogate while still driving the production AntiSurgeController through its real surge-margin interface.

    Description
    AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark()
    Builds the benchmark with a default 10% surge-margin set point and representative PI tuning.

  • Constructor Details

    • AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark

      public AntiSurgeDynamicBenchmark()
      Builds the benchmark with a default 10% surge-margin set point and representative PI tuning.

  • Method Details

    • run

      public void run(boolean controllerActive)
      Runs the transient and records the surge-margin and valve-opening histories.
      Parameters:
      controllerActive - whether the anti-surge controller is allowed to act; when false the recycle valve stays closed (open-loop reference case)

    • isSurgeAvoided

      public boolean isSurgeAvoided()
      Indicates whether the machine was kept out of surge during the last run.
      Returns:
      true if the minimum distance to surge stayed at or above zero

    • getMinimumSurgeMargin

      public double getMinimumSurgeMargin()
      Gets the minimum distance to surge observed during the last run.
      Returns:
      the minimum distance to surge (dimensionless), or NaN if no run has executed

    • getMaximumValveOpening

      public double getMaximumValveOpening()
      Gets the maximum recycle valve opening observed during the last run.
      Returns:
      the maximum valve opening in percent, or NaN if no run has executed

    • getSurgeMarginTrace

      public double[] getSurgeMarginTrace()
      Gets the recorded distance-to-surge history of the last run.
      Returns:
      a copy of the surge-margin trace, or null if no run has executed

    • getValveOpeningTrace

      public double[] getValveOpeningTrace()
      Gets the recorded valve-opening history of the last run.
      Returns:
      a copy of the valve-opening trace in percent, or null if no run has executed

    • getController

      public AntiSurgeController getController()
      Gets the anti-surge controller under test.
      Returns:
      the anti-surge controller

    • setInitialMargin

      public void setInitialMargin(double initialMargin)
      Sets the initial distance to surge at the start of the transient.
      Parameters:
      initialMargin - the initial distance to surge (dimensionless, should be positive)

    • setDisturbanceRate

      public void setDisturbanceRate(double disturbanceRate)
      Sets the disturbance rate eroding the surge margin.
      Parameters:
      disturbanceRate - the disturbance rate per second (must be non-negative)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the rate is negative

    • setRecycleAuthority

      public void setRecycleAuthority(double recycleAuthority)
      Sets the recycle authority restoring the margin at a fully open valve.
      Parameters:
      recycleAuthority - the recycle authority per second (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the authority is not positive

    • setTimeStep

      public void setTimeStep(double timeStep)
      Sets the integration time step.
      Parameters:
      timeStep - the time step in seconds (must be positive)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the time step is not positive

    • setNumberOfSteps

      public void setNumberOfSteps(int numberOfSteps)
      Sets the number of integration steps in the transient.
      Parameters:
      numberOfSteps - the number of steps (must be at least 1)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fewer than one step is requested
